final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original illustration of the Shangyuan Lantern Festival in Tang Dynasty Chang'an city, a sweeping panoramic handscroll scene lit by ten thousand lanterns.

Style/medium: gongbi (工笔) meticulous brushwork with heavy mineral pigments (重彩), horizontal handscroll (长卷) format, referencing the refined court painting tradition of Tang and Song dynasty court artists
Composition: wide panoramic horizontal sweep, bird's-eye to mid-level view capturing the full breadth of Chang'an's main boulevard and surrounding structures, layered depth with foreground crowds, mid-ground lantern towers, and distant city gates
Subject: vast Tang Dynasty lantern festival scene — thousands of elaborately crafted flower lanterns (花灯) in silk, paper, and lacquer, suspended from poles and archways; richly dressed Tang-era citizens and court figures strolling among the lights; decorated palace facades and city walls
Scene/backdrop: Chang'an city at night during the Shangyuan festival, grand central avenue lined with willow trees, imperial architecture with upturned eaves silhouetted against a deep indigo sky
Lighting/mood: ten thousand lanterns casting warm amber and gold glow across the scene, soft pools of red, yellow, and green light overlapping, festive and luminous atmosphere, night sky illuminated from below
Color palette: deep indigo and ink-black night sky as ground, warm gold and vermillion lantern light as dominant accent, malachite green and azurite blue in architectural details, white lead highlights on silk garments
Materials/textures: fine brushline outlines (白描) with layered mineral pigment washes, visible silk or paper scroll texture, gold leaf accents on lanterns and architectural trim
Avoid: watercolor looseness, impressionistic blur, modern illustration flatness, anachronistic elements, overexposed bloom effects, generic fantasy glow
